mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, it signals a cooling labor market that reduces aggregate consumer demand expectations, causing equity prices to decline over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >70% of observations where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicates robust corporate revenue growth without triggering monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5, or positive returns occur in less than 55% of triggered months over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and margin expansion, driving strong positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative alpha during recovery phases, with forward 5-day returns averaging below zero in >60% of recovery period observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 24-month window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >65% of months where housing starts fall below 1.2 million, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating inflation indicate a restrictive monetary policy stance that compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power with a coefficient t-statistic below 1.5, or positive returns dominate in >60% of high-rate-high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during a mid-cycle expansion phase reflects broad-based economic strength and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >55% of mid-cycle expansion quarters, or the GDP growth signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10) in walk-forward testing.
